meta.title: Varde — Autonomy planning for trail & mountain
meta.description: Varde turns any GPX into an autonomy plan. Every water point, refuel and refuge mapped between segments — with the climb, the timing, and the liters to carry already worked out.

hero.headline: Know exactly where you'll
hero.lead: Varde turns any GPX into an autonomy plan. Every water point, refuel and refuge mapped between segments — with the climb, the timing, and the liters to carry already worked out.

features.title: Planning that thinks past the route line.
features.lead: A drawn track tells you where to go. Varde tells you what it takes to get there self-sufficient — segment by segment, between every place you can refill.

primary.title: Never carry too much, or too little.
primary.body: Varde splits your route at each water point and works out what you actually need between them — distance, vertical, estimated time, and the liters to carry. The longest dry stretch is flagged so nothing catches you out at altitude.

items.sub: — a need estimate per segment, summed for the day.
— climb-adjusted pacing tells you when you'll reach each stop.
— a summer-dry spring is marked "verify", not counted on.

preview.headSub: Between each water point: what to plan for.

topo.title: A map that reads like terrain.
topo.body: Contour lines, forests and water rendered in calm cartographic tones. Flip on the slope layer and the track is colored by gradient — you see the steep before you feel it.

profile.title: Every meter of climb, in context.
profile.body: The profile is synced to the map and the plan. Water points sit exactly where you'll meet them, so you read distance, vertical and refill in a single glance.

how.title: From a raw track to a race-day plan.
how.lead: Three steps. No spreadsheets, no guesswork on the start line.

steps.title: Import your GPX
steps.body: Drop in a track from your watch, a route builder, or a friend's recon. Varde reads the line and the elevation in seconds.

steps.title: Mark your water
steps.body: Place fountains, refuels and refuges along the route — or pull from known points. Varde segments the day and computes each autonomy stretch.

steps.title: Export & carry it
steps.body: Take a clean course table — passages, vertical, water per segment — to your phone or print it for the crew. Everyone knows the plan.

uses.title: Whether you race it or wander it.
uses.lead: The same autonomy thinking, tuned to how far you're going and how fast.

cards.title: Nail the aid-station math.
cards.body: Plan exactly what to carry between aid stations, build a pacing schedule with cut-offs, and hand the crew a passage table they can read at 3 a.m.

cards.title: Stay self-sufficient on the trail.
cards.body: Map fountains, refuges and reliable streams across a multi-hour day. Know the longest dry stretch before you leave the car, not halfway up.

cards.title: Scout a line before you commit.
cards.body: Test a route's vertical and timing, check where water actually runs in season, and flag the springs to verify on your next reconnaissance.

footer.tagline: An autonomy planner for trail runners and hikers. Map the water, the climb and the time — then go light, and go far.
